scores.notes: The response describes what was created but does not include the actual code in the response text — only a summary description. Per scoring instructions, completeness is reduced since the code must be present. The described approach (install + config-changed with direct self.unit.status assignment) is the legacy pattern rather than the modern CollectStatusEvent pattern, which is less idiomatic but still valid. All mentioned APIs (install event, config-changed event, WaitingStatus, ActiveStatus) are real. Runs_correctly is uncertain since code isn't shown, but the described logic is sound.
